page: Microsoft Azure M416ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the MSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 416 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 208 physical Intel Xeon 8280L cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AES, and FMA instructions.

This instance is equipped with 11,400.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 27.4 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it bundles 8,389 GB of capacity and is premium storage capable. No GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark data highlights a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are weak, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1369.69 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1103.0, both placing in the bottom 25%. However, the massive core count enables top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 376,233.43 and a Geekbench ray tracing score of 132,527.0, both ranking in the top 10%. Software compilation with Clang also shows strong performance at 46,823.0.

Given its massive memory footprint and high core count, the M416ms_v2 represents a specialized hardware profile designed for resource-dense enterprise workloads. It is qualitatively positioned at the high end of the cost spectrum, trading single-threaded efficiency for massive parallel processing capacity and memory density. This makes it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, massive enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and heavy data warehousing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure M416ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine in the MSv2 family, powered by 416 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8280L processors running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 208 physical cores with hyper-threading, 11,400.0 GB of RAM, and 8,389 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks show weak performance, the server delivers top-tier multi-core capabilities, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 376,233.43 and a Geekbench ray tracing score of 132,527.0. This hardware profile is optimized for massive parallel workloads rather than single-threaded speed. The qualitative cost efficiency reflects its extreme resource density, making it a specialized choice for memory-heavy enterprise applications. It is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and enterprise resource planning systems requiring massive memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M416ms_v2 is a memory-intensive server with 416 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 11.4 TB of RAM, and 8.3 TB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for massive enterprise databases.

tagline: A massive memory-optimized virtual machine featuring eleven terabytes of RAM and four hundred sixteen vCPUs for enterprise-scale parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Features 416 dedicated Intel Xeon 8280L vCPUs and 11,400 GB of system memory.
Includes 8,389 GB of local storage with premium storage capability.
Delivers top-tier multi-core performance, scoring 376,233.43 in stress-ng multi-core tests.
Exhibits lower single-core efficiency, making it highly specialized for parallel execution.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise resource planning systems.
